Admission requirements for Direct Entry candidates
responses: For UTME candidates, admission into 100 level at the University of Abuja requires credit passes in five (5) subjects, including English Language and Mathematics, obtained in not more than two (2) sittings, with three (3) credits relevant to your chosen course. You also need an acceptable UTME score and must pass the university's Post-UTME screening.

For Direct Entry, you need at least two (2) 'A' Level passes (or equivalent qualifications such as ND, HND, NCE, IJMB or a first degree), plus five (5) credit passes including English Language and Mathematics at O'Level (WAEC, NECO, GCE or NABTEB).

The University of Abuja's baseline UTME cut-off mark is 180 for most courses, but each department sets its own departmental cut-off, which may be higher. For example, Medicine and Surgery (MBBS) typically requires around 230. Please confirm the exact cut-off for your specific course on the admission portal, as it may be revised each session.

To apply, first choose the University of Abuja as your institution on your JAMB CAOS profile, then register for the university's Post-UTME screening at portal.uniabuja.edu.ng once it opens. Admission is based on your UTME score, Post-UTME performance, and O'Level results.
You can check your admission status on the JAMB CAOS portal or the University of Abuja admission portal at portal.uniabuja.edu.ng using your JAMB registration number.

How do I know my Post-UTME screening result?
Is there a form fee for the screening?
What is the minimum score for Post-UTME screening?
responses: Post-UTME screening registration is done online at portal.uniabuja.edu.ng — click on 'click here to apply' to begin. Keep checking the portal and JAMB CAOS for the exact registration window for your session, as dates change every year.

To be eligible for Post-UTME screening, UTME candidates must meet the UTME cut-off mark and departmental cut-off, have at least 5 credits including English Language and Mathematics in not more than two sittings, and must have uploaded their O'Level results on the JAMB portal. Direct Entry candidates must have their A'Level results uploaded and verified on JAMB (note: NABTEB and JUPEB A'Level results are generally not accepted for Direct Entry at UniAbuja).
The Post-UTME/Direct Entry screening registration fee has typically been N2,000. Please confirm the current fee on the official portal before making payment, as it can be reviewed each session.

Candidates who do not register for the Post-UTME screening exercise will not be considered for admission, even if they meet the UTME cut-off mark, so it's important to complete this step promptly once the form opens.

There is usually a minimum age requirement (around 16 years) by a specified date in the admission session. Full eligibility criteria and any age cut-off dates are published on the portal when the screening form is released.

responses: The University of Abuja is organised into 14 faculties: Agriculture, Arts, Basic Medical Sciences, Clinical Sciences, Communication and Media Studies, Education, Engineering, Environmental Sciences, Law, Management Sciences, Nursing and Allied Health Sciences, Science, Social Sciences, and Veterinary Medicine.

Yes, the University of Abuja offers Medicine and Surgery (MBBS) through its Faculties of Basic Medical Sciences and Clinical Sciences, in affiliation with the University of Abuja Teaching Hospital in Gwagwalada.

Popular faculties include Science (covering courses like Computer Science, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Mathematics), Engineering, Law, Management Sciences, Social Sciences, Education, Agriculture, and Environmental Sciences.

For the complete, up-to-date list of departments and specific programmes under each faculty, please check the official University of Abuja website at uniabuja.edu.ng/faculties, as new programmes are occasionally added.
You can ask me about a specific faculty (for example, 'does UniAbuja offer engineering?') and I'll let you know if it's available.

responses: Hostel accommodation at the University of Abuja is not compulsory, but both fresh and returning students can apply. Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis, so it's best to apply as early as the portal opens.

To apply, log in to the student portal at portal.uniabuja.edu.ng with your matric number and password, select a hostel, make payment, and then take your payment proof to the Student Affairs Division for documentation and allocation.

Hostel fees vary by hostel type and are reviewed each session. As a guide: New Male/Female Hostels (Main Campus) have been around N40,000, Old Male/Female Hostels around N25,000, and the International Students' Hostel around N100,000. Please confirm the current fees on the portal before paying.
Once you book a hostel space, you typically have about one week to complete payment — unpaid bookings are usually revoked and reassigned to other students, so pay promptly after booking.
Hostel space is limited, so not every student is guaranteed a room, particularly some returning students in higher levels. Priority is often given to fresh students and those who apply early.

patterns: Tell me about screening after admission
What do I do after I get admission?

What next after I see my name on the admission list?
How do I complete my clearance process?
What is required for physical screening?
Do I need my JAMB documents for clearance?
How do I pay school fees and register courses?
responses: After admission, new students must attend a physical Credential Screening exercise at the Permanent Site, where your original certificates (WAEC/NECO, JAMB result, birth certificate, LG/indigene certificate, passport photographs) are verified against your JAMB and application records. Bring both originals and photocopies of all documents.

After your credentials are verified, you proceed to pay your school fees and register your courses on the student portal (portal.uniabuja.edu.ng). Your matriculation number is generated only after school fees have been paid and courses registered — it is not issued automatically at admission.

The Matriculation Ceremony is a mandatory event for all newly admitted students, formally confirming your enrolment and commitment to the university's academic rules and code of conduct. Watch the portal and official announcements for the exact date and venue for your session.

In summary, the typical order after admission is: (1) credential/document screening, (2) school fee payment, (3) course registration on the portal, (4) matric number generation, then (5) matriculation ceremony. Always confirm the exact checklist and dates published for your specific session, as these can change.
Keep your phone and email active during this period — the university communicates screening venues, dates, and any changes to registered applicants through the portal, email, and SMS.
